Thisphasedisplays an incompressible totaldensity, although the relative density remain compressible. We use strong-coupling and Monte Carlo calculationsto show that this phase exists for abroad parameter range ofthe interaction strengths and mass ratio. The phase is stable against thermal excitations up to critical temperatureswhich exceedthe typical fermionic Mott transition temperature inan ultracoldgas by at least one order of magnitude. We find that the disordered insulator phase displaysuniversal behavior, as it can bedescribed bya phenomenological Landau-Ginzburg-Wilson theory with two fields: The total density and the relative density. texts.full_text: like loving the idea of a person. I loved that she would be my daughter. I loved that I would be a mommy. I loved her smile, and her fat face. It's true, it's a fat face. And I love it. I loved her expressive eyes. I loved her little broken heart. All before we knew her, I loved these things. And when we got her, I still loved those things, but there was more. I loved the way she held my hand. I loved the way she laughed. I loved the way she had become real to us, a daughter. I loved the way she enjoyed every new experience with abandoned joy, a clap, a squeal, and sometimes a little dance. Because when you are really, really excited, a little dance is always appropriate. But there were still times when I wasn't quite sure. Times when I felt like a babysitter, times when it just didn't seem real yet. Times when I wasn't sure I was going to make it. All through those times, I was getting to know little M even better. And there was more to love. I love her abandoned love of food. I love the face she makes when she is thinking. I love the prideshe shows off whenever someone compliments one of toys or books (even if it's something every other kid has). I even loved how, for a long time there, everything was "mine". Because that meant those things were hers and in her world everyone gets to share. Even though she didn't always want them back! There are so many more little quirks about my girl that make me love her fiercely every single day but this post is already too long for a blog I don't even know if anyone reads (hi, Mom!). texts.full_text: in C++. Add two numbers without using Arithmetic Operators We wish to add two numbers without using any arithmetic operators like +, -, *, / etc. One way of doing this is by using bitwise operators. If we have two single-bit binary numbers then we can find their sum by simple half adder logic. That is we get the sum by performing bitwise XOR(exclusive-OR) operation and the carry by performing bitwise AND operation. We can extend this logic to perform the addition of any length binary numbers by using a full adder logic. We can use the following formula to add two numbers a and b: 1. Carry = a AND b 2. texts.full_text: s more than happy to pass my love of this movie universe down to my son…and it’s one of the things that we happily get to bond over. He was almost as excited as I was when the new Star Wars: Episode 7 trailer was released recently. We’ve both been counting down the months until the time arrives when we can experience new Star Wars memories together for the first time. To prepare for doing that, I decided that we should watch some Star Wars: The Clone Wars on Netflix. The series is set in the fictional Star Wars galaxy during the three years between the prequel films Attack of the Clones and Revenge of the Sith. What makes it fun for me is the fact that the stories are considered part of the Star Wars canon, so they “really took place” and are officially part of the new Star Wars expanded universe that launches with Episode VII in December. The hundreds of books and video games and comic books that have been released over the past 30+ years are just stories but are not considered part of the actual Star Wars

texts.full_text: Yamaha Corporation of America has awarded two scholarships to members of the Madison Scouts Drum and Bugle Corps. The scholarships, each valued at $1,000, were presented to trumpet player Emily Johnson and mellophone player Alexandra Lee during the corps' annual banquet. "We are incredibly grateful to Yamaha for their continued support of the Madison Scouts and for providing these scholarships to our deserving members," said Chris Komnick, Corps Director of the Madison Scouts. "Emily and Alexandra have shown exceptional dedication and talent, and these scholarships will help them further their education and musical careers." Emily Johnson is a music education major at the University of Wisconsin-Madison and has been a member of the Madison Scouts for three years. Alexandra Lee is a music performance major at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and has been with the corps for two years. "Yamaha is committed to supporting music education and providing opportunities for young musicians to grow and succeed," said Troy Wollwage, Marketing Manager for Yamaha Corporation of America. "We are proud to partner with the Madison Scouts and to recognize Emily and Alexandra for their outstanding achievements." The Madison Scouts, founded in 1938, are one of the oldest and most prestigious drum and bugle corps in the world. They are a member of Drum Corps International (DCI) and have won multiple DCI World Championships. For more information about the Madison Scouts, visit www.madisonscouts.org. Rumours say that his mother Jane, daughter of John Hookham (senior)of London, passed away on January 3rd, 1826 at the age of ninety-two years old and was buried in the churchyard. Following this, Frere lost his dear wife Anne Elizabeth Parker a few weeks later. His sister Catherine also succumbed to her illness during that same month, leaving John Hookham Frere surrounded by sorrow. Risk of bias assessment was conducted using the Cochrane risk-of-bias tool. Effect sizes were calculated for each outcome and meta-analyses were performed where appropriate. Results The search identified 12 eligible studies, all of which were included in the qualitative synthesis and 9 of which provided data for meta-analysis. The MHFA program was associated with significant improvements in mental health first aid knowledge, recognition of mental disorders, treatment knowledge, and confidence to help. There were also reductions in stigma and social distance towards people with mental illness. However, there was no significant effect on the actual provision of mental health first aid. Conclusions The MHFA training program appears to be effective in improving mental health knowledge, reducing stigma, and increasing confidence in helping behaviours related to mental health issues among participants. texts.full_text: f creme rinses or conditioning treatments. Problems arise from the limited compatibility of anionic detergents with commercial cationic conditioning agents which precipitate out of solution in shampoo formulations. Shampoo formulations have employed conventional anionic surfactants such as sodium lauryl sulfate, ammonium lauryl sulfate, ammonium lauryl ether sulfates and sodium lauryl ether sulfates which have been found to be incompatible with most cationic conditioning at effective concentration levels. Additionally, reproducible thickening for formulations containing anionic detergents such as sodium 12-alkyl sulphate has proven difficult due in part to the fact that these surfactants are highly soluble and thus do not provide a high viscosity even when concentrated. The present invention provides compositions which overcome many of the problems associated with conventional hair treatment products. The composition is an aqueous, thickened shampoo formulation containing both cationic conditioning agents as well as effective levels (1-5%) of sodium 12-hydroxy lauryl sulphate and other alkyl ether sulfates. The present invention provides a novel combination which allows the use in hair treatment formulations at high concentrations of anionic detergents such as sodium 12-lauroyl sulfate, ammonium 12-lauric acid ester sulphonate or sodium ethoxylated lauryl sulphate with cationic conditioning agents. The present invention also provides a novel combination which allows the use in hair treatment formulations at high concentrations of anionic detergents such as sodium 12-lauroyl sulfate, ammonium 12-lauric acid ester sulphonate or sodium ethoxylated lauryl sulphate with cationic conditioning agents. texts.full_text: —The Core Program TNIP Expert is the latest version of the main TNIP program. It has a very different user interface to the previous version (TNIPv3.6). If you have been using previous versions of TNIP, it is advisable that you obtain TNIP Expert since it is much easier to use. TNIP Expert is a tool which takes data either from Noise and Flight Path Monitoring systems, or from noise modelling studies carried out using the US Federal Aviation Administration's Integrated Noise Model (INM), and produces ‘real’ information about aircraft noise. It can be used to produce a range of flight path and aircraft movement based noise descriptors or to produce and manipulate conventional noise contours. TNIP Expert enables the user to ‘look inside’ noise contours and easily see the number and types of aircraft that have been allocated to the flight tracks which underpin the contours. The allocations of aircraft to tracks can be altered in a way that allows the carrying out of rapid ‘what-if’ analyses. texts.full_text: to be harder than Act IV Nightmare And so on. Does Diablo II work the same way? If so, how is the Lord of Destruction expansion pack handled? Is Normal Act V intended to be as hard as Nightmare Act I, or was Nightmare re-tuned such that Act I Nigare in LoD would feel harder than it did before? A: Diablo 2's difficulty scaling works differently from Diablo III. In D3 the game is designed around a single progression path where each act gets progressively more difficult and you are expected to play through all of them sequentially. In Diablo II, there was no expectation that players will progress in any particular order or even at all past Act I Normal (which we considered our "main" difficulty). The other difficulties were intended as optional challenges for those who wanted a greater challenge. As such the game is designed around multiple progression paths where each act can be played on its own and there are no expectations about what comes next. The Lord of Destruction expansion pack was handled similarly to how Diablo III handles additional acts: it added new content that could stand alone or fit into existing playthroughs as desired by players. The difficulty scaling for LoD is not sequential like in D3 but rather parallel with multiple progression paths available at any given time (e.g., Act V Normal, Act IV Nightmare). Q: How does Diablo II's difficulty scaling work? A: In Diablo 2 the game was designed around a single progression path where each act got progressively more difficult and you were expected to play through all of them sequentially. The other difficulties were intended as optional challenges for those who wanted greater challenge; there wasn't any expectation that players would progress in particular order or even past Act I Normal (which we considered our "main" difficulty). The reduced cost and liability of 401(k) plans appeal to employers. 403(b) A qualified retirement plan similar to the 401(k), but available to employees of public schools and certain tax-exempt organizations. We postulate that apical and basolateral plasma membrane proteins are segregated after leaving the Golgi apparatus into discrete vesicles that interact differently with the microtubular apparatus of epithelial cells, and with different plasma membrane domains. We will examine physiologically important molecules such as proton pumps, anion exchangers, and glucose transporters under conditions that alter intracellular trafficking, including microtubule disruption, acid-base manipulations and manoeuvres that block protein export from the Golgi to the cell surface. We will ask whether membrane and secreted proteins ride in the same vesicles, and whether lipid-anchored membrane proteins are handled differently by epithelial cells. Finally, we will examine the subcellular distribution and assembly of the proton pump using specific antibodies. 2) to examine the role of GTP-binding proteins in transport vesicle function. texts.full_text: layered configuration. This mechanism provides flexibility in managing and applying configurations across different environments and systems. When you use `os-apply-config` with the `-t` flag, you specify the directory containing the templates that should be applied. The tool will look for configuration files based on the paths provided in `/var/run/os-collect-config/os_config_files.json` or as specified by the `OS_CONFIG_FILES_PATH` environment variable. If you prefer to directly specify the configuration files, you can use the `OS_CONFIG_FILES` environment variable. In this case, you need to list the paths to the JSON files, separated by colons. The tool will merge these JSON files, with files listed later having precedence over earlier ones, allowing for fine-grained control over configuration settings. Here's a step-by-step breakdown of how to use `os-apply-config`: 1. Prepare Templates: Ensure your templates are organized in a directory, such as `/home/me/my_templates`. 2. Default Configuration: By default, `os-apply-config` will look for configuration files in `/var/run/os-collect-config/os_config_files.json`. 3. Override Configuration Path: If needed, change the configuration file path using the `–os-config-files` command line switch or set the `OS_CONFIG_FILES_PATH` environment variable. 4. Directly Specify Configuration Files: Use the `OS_CONFIG_FILES` environment variable to list specific configuration files. Ensure they are JSON files with a root mapping structure, and separate paths with colons. 5. Apply Templates: Run the command with `sudo` to apply the templates: ```bash sudo os-apply-config -t /home/me/my_templates ``` This approach allows you to manage configurations efficiently, especially in environments where configurations need

texts.full_text: I would like to have a table view with each row pointing to another screen. I thought I could accomplish this in storyboard by just control dragging each statment cell onto the appropriate controller. I have been unable to get it working though, so I'm hoping someone here has experience with something similar? A: You can do that using the storyboard but you would have to manually connect the segue from each row. A more elegant solution is to use a tabbarcontroller inside another viewcontroller. The tabbarcontroller will be embedded into the mainviewcontroller which in turn will contain your tableview and each row of your tableview will be segued into the appropriate tab. Here's a video on how to do that: And here are the steps for embedding a view controller into another one (which you will also need): 1. In Storyboard, Control-drag from View Controller 2 to Tab Bar Controller. 2. Select "Embed Segue" from the popup menu. 3. The Tab Bar Controller is now embedded in View Controller 2. I put flank steak to the test with the sous vide, and it came out… wait for it… perfectly cooked all the way through and all I did was plug in a few temperatures and let the sous vide work its magic. The sous vide controls the temperature and cook of the food, but the marinade is what controls the FLAVOR! In this video, I show you my family’s favorite easy steak marinade recipe. We are known for this because we always make it for our dinner parties, and it is always a crowd pleaser. texts.full_text: characteristics typical of a small-world network, such as high clustering coefficients and short average path lengths. This structure suggests an efficient balance between localized interactions and global connectivity, which is essential for rapid metabolic response and robustness in the face of perturbations. High clustering coefficients indicate that many nodes (metabolic reactions) are interconnected within local clusters, reflecting modular organization where related pathways operate together. Short average path lengths mean that any two enzymes or metabolites can be connected through a relatively small number of steps, enabling quick signal transmission and metabolic coordination across the network. These properties imply that perturbations in one part of glycolysis or the TCA cycle can rapidly affect other parts

texts.full_text: cannot be matched simply by using smaller versions of currently available designs. An effective compact cooling system (Holtzapple and Allen, 1983) should preferably satisfy the dual requirements of a high coefficient of performance and a light and compact design. One example of an effective and useful microclimate system preferably would be able to remove at least 120 W of heat while consuming no more than 50 W of electrical power for at least about 4 hours of operation. This would suggest that for this particular example the microclimate system would have a coefficient of performance, or heat removal to power input ratio, of 2.4. In conventional designs, the requirements of a high coefficient of performance and a light and compact design typically work against each other. Current cooling methods, such as thermoelectric cooling and traditional refrigeration cycles, have a high coefficient of performance and efficient design size within certain cooling ranges. While thermoelectric coolers have a coefficient of performance close to 1.0 and a very small volumetric design relative to the cooling capacity when operating in the 10 to 100 watt range, the coefficient of performance of commercially available thermo-electric devices tend be at or below 0.6 when applied to higher cooling capacities. In personal or portable cooling units heat removal rates of this range are inadequate. An alternative to mitigating the lack of performance and increase cooling capacity would be to use more units in series or parallel, thus increasing the overall size and weight of the cooling unit to beyond

To address this hypothesis, we propose three specific aims: This project will utilize cellular and animal models of illness/injury and employ chemical/genetic inhibition of enzymes in these pathways to address the following specific aims: 1) Evaluate the role of GLN transport and metabolism via the O-GlcNAc pathway in stress/injury. 2) Evaluate via promoter truncation which promoter regions are key for GLN-mediated HSP expression. Then, determine the effect of GLN on nuclear translocation and transactivation of key transcription factors responsible for HSP expression. 3) Evaluate effects of GLN transport, metabolism, and transcriptional activation on the following cellular and in vivo endpoints: a) HSP expression (multiple families of HSPs), b) tissue/cellular injury, and c) cell volume. This project will elucidate how GLN induces HSP expression in clinical illness and injury. We believe GLN will be able to be administered as a pharmacologic agent prior to surgery or at onset of critical illness/tissue injury (with admittance to ICU/emergency room) to enhance HSP expression and improve survival. texts.full_text: am Road, Aberdeen Tunnel and Nam Fung Road through Wong Chuk Hang. Nam Fung Road connects Deep Water Bay Road, which also connects Wong Nai Chung Gap Road to Happy Valley. A bridge links Aberdeen with Ap Lei Chau over Aberdeen Harbour. The proposed MTR South Island Line (West Section) will have stations in this area. There are regular ferries to Yung Shue Wan and Sok Kwu Wan on Lamma Island. Transport to and from the boats in the harbour is usually provided by sampans. These can also be hired to Lamma. Food Aberdeen Jumbo Floating Restaurant is a favoured tourist spot. Looking like a floating palace of imperial China, it has attracted over 30 million visitors since opening in 1976. Some of the famous diners include Queen Elizabeth II, John Wayne, Tom Cruise, Chow Yun Fat and Gong Li. It has also been featured in many Hong Kong and Hollywood movies. texts.full_text: and NHUC. Furthermore, fenretinide, TRAIL or the combination of both agents was not cytotoxic to either of these non-malignant cell lines, indicating that the combination of fenretinide and TRAIL may have minimal toxicity. Interestingly, FAS was downregulated by fenretinide, and both DcRs were strongly increased in NHUC. We also observed that fenretinide upregulated TRAIL DcR in ESFT cells, which supports previous data in which TRAIL DcRs are upregulated alongside DR by apoptotic stimuli such as UV ([@bib26]), doxorubicin ([@bib52]) and oxaplatin ([@bib43]). Collectively, these observations suggest a possible mechanism to evade apoptosis, the higher DcR to DR ratio being likely to drive cells towards a survival pathway. Cytoprotective roles for DcR have previously been elucidated and are attributed to DcR-mediated stimulation of the Akt, ERK ([@bib36]) and NF-*κ*B ([@bib9]) survival pathways, competitive binding of TRAIL to DcR1 inhibiting DR-induced DISC formation and DcR2 forming ligand-independent complexes with DR5 at the DISC to inhibit DR4 corecruitment and caspase activation ([@bib2]). Our work demonstrates that the combination of fenretinide and DR ligands, actinomycin D and TRAIL and etoposide used in combination with either TRAIL or FasL may be advantageous for the treatment of ESFT cells. As DR ligands transduce apoptotic signals through different pathways to those of chemotherapy and irradiation, these combinations may augment patient response to either drug alone and so be more effective. texts.full_text: r Taylor Swift, I have read your open letter to Apple where you give your reasons for refusing to allow your album ‘1989′ to be included on their forthcoming Apple Music streaming service. (For reference: http://taylorswift.tumblr.com/post/122071902085/to-apple-love-Taylor, I understand your frustration and the passion you have for supporting artists in this ever-changing music industry landscape. Your stance on Apple's initial policy of not paying royalties during their three month free trial period is commendable; it highlights a genuine concern that many musicians share today—the struggle to maintain fair compensation amidst evolving consumption habits and technology advancements like streaming services. Your influence has the power to spark meaningful conversations about these issues, encouraging companies such as Apple (and others in similar positions) to reconsider their policies for better alignment with artists' interests. It is heartening that your voice was heard so quickly; within hours of posting your letter on Tumblr and Twitter, Eddy Cue from Apple announced a change: they will now pay royalties during the free trial period after all! This swift response underscores both how crucial it can be for artists to speak up about their concerns—and also shows that companies are listening.
